what_it_does: A bash 'drive handle' script that mints a design_client JWT for a throwaway test user and exercises the connector Edge Function's security gates A–H against a local Supabase stack — RLS reads/writes, fail-closed tool allow-listing, an inlet ingest, audit-row inspection, and an audit-before-dispatch fail-closed test.
observations: Targets Edge Function at 127.0.0.1:8773/mcp and Supabase API at 127.0.0.1:15421.
Embeds the supabase-demo ANON and SERVICE_ROLE JWTs inline as variables.
Mints a token via password grant for design-test@company.local / TestPass123! and exits with FAIL if no token returned.
Defines a call() helper that POSTs JSON-RPC tools/call requests with the minted bearer token.
Gate A: lists channels expecting only the granted shared 'design' channel via RLS.
Gate B: posts to granted 'design' expecting success.
Gate E/F: posts to non-granted 'internal-only-fake' expecting a 403 RLS dispatch error.
Gate G: calls cc_clone (not in client allow-list) expecting a REFUSED isError.
Inlet test: calls claude_design ingest_bundle expecting success.
Reads connector_audit rows using the service_role key.
Gate H: revokes INSERT on connector_audit via docker exec psql, sends a post (expecting audit-pending-failed and NO persisted post), compares channel_posts counts before/after, then re-grants INSERT.
standouts: Gate H proves audit-before-dispatch: if the audit insert can't happen, the write must not persist (verified by before/after row counts).
Gate G proves fail-closed tool allow-listing rather than relying solely on RLS.
Directly manipulates the live DB grants via docker exec to simulate audit failure, then restores them.
conventions: Follows the lettered-gate (A–H) verification convention referenced elsewhere in this design work.
Uses python3 inline one-liners to parse JSON from curl, a recurring pattern in these scripts.
Self-documents creds as LOCAL THROWAWAY, not boundary, not prod, gitignored.
concerns: Embeds service_role and anon JWTs plus a test password in plaintext (local/demo keys, but still in-repo).
Mutates database grants on a shared local DB mid-run; an aborted run before the re-grant would leave INSERT revoked.
set -u only (no -e/pipefail); a failing curl mid-script won't necessarily halt subsequent gates.
notes: A self-contained security smoke-test that drives the connector Edge Function through eight RLS/allow-list/audit gates against a local Supabase, including a destructive-then-restored grant toggle.
questions: What is the full set of gates and what do C and D cover (only A,B,E/F,G,H are explicitly labeled here)?
What is the connector's audit-before-dispatch implementation that gate H is validating?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (drive handle to mint a design_client JWT and exercise gates A–H) matches what the script does.
intention_signals: Trying to prove, against a live local stack, that the connector enforces channel RLS, refuses non-allow-listed tools, and will not persist a write when its audit record can't be written.
novelty: The audit-before-dispatch destructive test (revoke INSERT, confirm no persistence, re-grant) as an executable proof.
contribution: Contributes an executable security-gate verification harness for the connector Edge Function covering RLS, allow-list refusal, inlet ingest, and audit-before-dispatch.
summary_for_embedding: A bash script that mints a test design_client JWT and runs connector Edge Function security gates A–H (RLS, allow-list, ingest, audit-before-dispatch) against a local Supabase.
intention_for_embedding: To verify the connector's fail-closed security gates live against a local Supabase stack before trusting them.
